name: DAAD EPOS (Development-Related Postgraduate Courses)
summary: Full scholarships for a Master's (or in some cases PhD) at a German university on a development-related programme, aimed at professionals from developing and emerging countries.
benefits: Health, accident and personal liability insurance
Possible rent subsidy and family allowances

eligibility.question: Do you hold a Bachelor's degree (or equivalent)?
eligibility.failMessage: EPOS requires a completed first academic degree, normally a Bachelor's.

eligibility.question: How many years of relevant professional experience do you have?
eligibility.failMessage: Most EPOS courses require at least 2 years of relevant professional experience after your first degree.

eligibility.question: Did you graduate within the last 6 years?
eligibility.failMessage: EPOS normally expects your last degree to have been obtained within the past 6 years.

eligibility.question: Can you meet the course language requirement (English or German)?
eligibility.failMessage: You must meet the language requirement of the specific course (e.g. IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught).

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters (EMJM)
summary: Prestigious, fully-funded joint Master's programmes delivered by a consortium of European (and sometimes non-European) universities, with study in at least two countries.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you hold) a Bachelor's degree before enrolment?
eligibility.failMessage: An EMJM requires a first higher-education degree before enrolment.

eligibility.question: Have you previously received an Erasmus Mundus scholarship?
eligibility.failMessage: You cannot hold more than one Erasmus Mundus scholarship.

eligibility.question: Can you meet the programme's English requirement?
eligibility.failMessage: Most EMJM programmes require proof of English (e.g. IELTS 6.5).

source.name: Erasmus+ — Erasmus Mundus Joint Masters catalogue

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Chevening Scholarships
summary: The UK government's flagship fully-funded award for one-year Master's degrees, for emerging leaders from Chevening-eligible countries who will return home afterwards.
benefits: Full tuition fees for a one-year UK Master's
Return economy airfare to the UK
Arrival and departure allowances; visa and some travel costs

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of a Chevening-eligible country?
eligibility.failMessage: Chevening is open only to citizens of eligible countries — check the list on the official site.

eligibility.question: Do you hold an undergraduate degree that qualifies for a UK Master's?
eligibility.failMessage: You need an undergraduate degree to enter a UK postgraduate course.

eligibility.question: Do you have at least 2 years (about 2,800 hours) of work experience?
eligibility.failMessage: Chevening normally requires roughly 2 years of work experience.

eligibility.question: Will you return to your home country for at least 2 years after the award?
eligibility.failMessage: Chevening requires you to return home for a minimum of two years after your studies.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Commonwealth Master's Scholarships
summary: Fully-funded UK Master's scholarships for talented citizens of low- and middle-income Commonwealth countries who could not otherwise afford to study in the UK.
benefits: Thesis, study travel and arrival allowances

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of (or refugee in) an eligible Commonwealth country?
eligibility.failMessage: Open only to citizens of eligible Commonwealth countries.

eligibility.question: Do you hold at least an upper second-class (2:1) honours undergraduate degree?
eligibility.failMessage: You normally need at least a 2:1 honours degree (or a lower degree plus a relevant Master's).

eligibility.question: Are you unable to afford to study in the UK without this scholarship?
eligibility.failMessage: These awards target those who could not otherwise afford UK study.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Australia Awards Scholarships
summary: Long-term, fully-funded study awards for citizens of partner developing countries to undertake undergraduate or postgraduate study at Australian institutions.
benefits: Return air travel and a one-off establishment allowance

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of a participating Australia Awards country?
eligibility.failMessage: Open only to citizens of participating partner countries.

eligibility.question: Are you living outside Australia and not an Australian citizen/PR?
eligibility.failMessage: You must not be a citizen or permanent resident of Australia and must apply from your home country.

eligibility.question: Will you leave Australia for at least 2 years after your studies?
eligibility.failMessage: Recipients must return home for at least two years after completing the award.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Swedish Institute Scholarships for Global Professionals (SISGP)
summary: Fully-funded Master's scholarships in Sweden for professionals from a defined list of countries with work and leadership experience who want to drive change at home.
benefits: Tuition fees paid directly to the university
Membership of the SI Network for Future Global Leaders

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of an SISGP-eligible country?
eligibility.failMessage: Open only to citizens of the countries on the SISGP list.

eligibility.question: Do you have at least 3,000 hours of work/volunteer/leadership experience?
eligibility.failMessage: SISGP requires a minimum of 3,000 hours of relevant experience.

eligibility.question: Have you applied to (or been admitted to) an eligible Swedish Master's?
eligibility.failMessage: You must first apply to an eligible Master's through universityadmissions.se.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Holland Scholarship
provider: Dutch Ministry of Education + research universities
summary: A partial scholarship for non-EEA international students starting a Bachelor's or Master's at a participating Dutch research university or university of applied sciences.
benefits: €5,000 grant in the first year of study
Combinable with some other funding (check the institution)

eligibility.question: Are you a non-EEA national?
eligibility.failMessage: The Holland Scholarship is for students from outside the EEA.

eligibility.question: Will this be your first enrolment at a participating Dutch institution?
eligibility.failMessage: It is for new students enrolling for a full Bachelor's or Master's, not exchange.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: MEXT Scholarship (Japanese Government)
summary: The Japanese government's fully-funded scholarship for international students to study at Japanese universities, available via embassy recommendation or university recommendation.
benefits: No-cost Japanese language training where applicable

eligibility.question: Do you meet the age and academic requirements for your track (research/undergraduate)?
eligibility.failMessage: MEXT sets age and prior-education requirements that vary by track — check the official guidelines.

eligibility.question: Are you a national of a country that has diplomatic relations with Japan?
eligibility.failMessage: Applicants must hold the nationality of a country with diplomatic relations with Japan.

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded.

name: Global Korea Scholarship (GKS) — Graduate
summary: The Korean government's flagship award: full tuition, a monthly stipend, airfare and a year of Korean language training for Master's and PhD study in Korea, via embassy or university track.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Are you under 40 years of age?
eligibility.failMessage: GKS graduate applicants must generally be under 40.

eligibility.question: Is your cumulative GPA at least ~80% (2.64/4.0 or higher)?
eligibility.failMessage: GKS normally expects grades of 80% or above from your previous degree.

eligibility.question: Can you meet the programme's language requirement (e.g. IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught)?
eligibility.failMessage: You must meet the language requirement of the specific programme.

benefits: Round-trip airfare, settlement and research allowances
One year of Korean language training

deadlines.intake: 2027 graduate intake — deadlines vary by embassy/university (typically Feb–Mar)

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded from the official source (machine-compiled; deadlines vary by year and country — confirm on the official page).

name: Fulbright Foreign Student Program
summary: The U.S. government's flagship exchange: funds Master's or PhD study (and research) at U.S. universities for graduate students and young professionals from 160+ countries.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Does your country run a Fulbright Foreign Student competition?
eligibility.failMessage: Applications go through the Fulbright commission or U.S. embassy in your home country.

eligibility.question: Can you demonstrate strong English (TOEFL/IELTS)?
eligibility.failMessage: U.S. graduate admission and Fulbright both require proven English proficiency.

benefits: Tuition and required fees (per country agreement)

deadlines.intake: 2027–28 cycle — deadlines set per country (many fall Feb–Oct 2026)

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded from the official source (machine-compiled; deadlines vary by year and country — confirm on the official page).

name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars
summary: Fully funds up to three years of any graduate degree at Stanford — MS, PhD, MBA, MD or JD — plus a leadership development programme, for scholars from any country.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Did you earn your first degree within the last ~7 years?
eligibility.failMessage: Knight-Hennessy requires a first degree earned recently (rolling ~7-year window).

eligibility.question: Are you applying to (or enrolled in) a Stanford graduate programme?
eligibility.failMessage: You must separately apply and be admitted to a Stanford graduate programme.

benefits: Full tuition and fees for up to 3 years
Open to all nationalities and all Stanford graduate degrees

deadlines.intake: Autumn 2027 entry — application typically due early October 2026

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded from the official source (machine-compiled; deadlines vary by year and country — confirm on the official page).

name: Gates Cambridge Scholarship
summary: Full-cost awards for outstanding international (non-UK) applicants to pursue a postgraduate degree at the University of Cambridge, with a strong commitment to improving the lives of others.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of a country other than the UK?
eligibility.failMessage: Gates Cambridge is for applicants who are not UK citizens.

eligibility.question: Are you applying for a full-time postgraduate degree at Cambridge?
eligibility.failMessage: You apply via the University of Cambridge graduate application — there is no separate route.

benefits: One economy return airfare and visa costs

deadlines.intake: 2027 entry — US round Oct 2026; all other countries Dec 2026/Jan 2027

name: Rhodes Scholarship (Oxford)
summary: One of the oldest international awards: fully funds two or more years of postgraduate study at the University of Oxford for young leaders selected by country constituency.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Will you meet your constituency's age limit (generally 18–24, up to 27 in some)?
eligibility.failMessage: Rhodes has strict age limits that vary by constituency.

eligibility.question: Is there a Rhodes constituency for your country/region?
eligibility.failMessage: You must apply through an eligible Rhodes constituency.

benefits: All University and college fees at Oxford
Two economy flights (to and from the UK)
Visa and health surcharge costs covered

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded from the official source (machine-compiled; deadlines vary by year and country — confirm on the official page).

name: Clarendon Fund Scholarships (Oxford)
summary: Oxford's largest graduate scholarship scheme: ~200 new awards a year covering full fees and a generous living stipend, open to all nationalities and all subjects — no separate application.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Are you applying to an eligible Oxford graduate course by the December/January deadline?
eligibility.failMessage: Clarendon considers everyone who applies to Oxford by the relevant early deadline — there is no separate form.

eligibility.question: Is your academic record in the top tier (typically first-class / GPA 3.7+)?
eligibility.failMessage: Selection is purely academic — outstanding grades and research potential are expected.

benefits: Annual living stipend (at least the UKRI rate)
Open to every nationality and subject

deadlines.intake: 2027 entry — apply to Oxford by the Dec 2026 / Jan 2027 course deadline

name: GREAT Scholarships
summary: £10,000 towards one-year taught Master's tuition at participating UK universities, for students from selected countries — jointly funded by the UK government's GREAT campaign.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?

name: Vanier Canada Graduate Scholarships
summary: Canada's premier doctoral award: CAD 50,000 per year for three years for high-calibre PhD students (Canadian and international) nominated by their Canadian university.

eligibility.question: Are you pursuing (or applying to) a PhD at a Canadian university?
eligibility.failMessage: Vanier funds doctoral study only.

eligibility.question: Can you be nominated by the Canadian university where you'll study?
eligibility.failMessage: You cannot apply directly — the Canadian institution must nominate you.

eligibility.failMessage: Vanier limits how far into the PhD you can be at nomination.

Assessed on academic excellence, research potential and leadership

eligibility.failMessage: You must be applying directly from secondary school to first-year undergraduate study.

eligibility.failMessage: A school nomination is required before you complete the scholarship application.

name: Eiffel Excellence Scholarship
summary: France's flagship award for international Master's and PhD students in science, engineering, economics, law and political science — a monthly allowance plus travel, insurance and cultural benefits (tuition not included).

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Are you 25 or under (Master's) / 30 or under (PhD)?
eligibility.failMessage: Eiffel has strict age caps: 25 for the Master's track, 30 for the PhD track.

eligibility.question: Will a French institution submit your application?
eligibility.failMessage: Only French higher-education institutions can submit Eiffel applications — you cannot apply directly.

eligibility.question: Are you a non-French national?
eligibility.failMessage: Eiffel is reserved for non-French nationals.

benefits: International travel, local transport and insurance
Does not cover tuition (many programmes waive it for laureates)

name: Swiss Government Excellence Scholarships

eligibility.question: Do you hold a Master's degree (or will before the start date)?

eligibility.question: Were you born after the cut-off year stated in this year's call (roughly 35 or younger)?
eligibility.failMessage: FCS applies a birth-year cut-off that changes each call.

eligibility.failMessage: A letter from a Swiss professor willing to supervise you is mandatory.

name: Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships

summary: Full scholarships (tuition, living allowance, airfare, insurance) for students from eligible developing countries — mainly the Pacific, Asia, Africa and Latin America — to study at New Zealand institutions.

eligibility.failMessage: Applicants normally must reside in their country of citizenship when applying.

benefits: Support to return home after study

name: Research Training Program (RTP)
summary: The Australian government's main funding scheme for research degrees: fee offsets and stipends (~AUD 33,000+/yr, university-set) for domestic and international Master's-by-research and PhD candidates, administered by each university.

eligibility.question: Are you applying for a research degree (MPhil/PhD), not coursework?
eligibility.failMessage: RTP supports research degrees only — coursework Master's are not eligible.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Have you identified a supervisor and applied through an Australian university?
eligibility.failMessage: RTP is applied for via the university, usually alongside your admission application.

benefits: Tuition-fee offset for up to 4 years (PhD)

Some allowances for relocation and thesis costs

deadlines.intake: 2027 rounds — most universities close international rounds Aug–Oct 2026

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?

eligibility.question: Is your academic record excellent (roughly GPA 3.5/4.0 or better)?
eligibility.failMessage: Master Mind is explicitly merit-based; a very strong record is required.

eligibility.question: Have you applied for (or secured) admission to an eligible programme?
eligibility.failMessage: This award requires an application or offer at an eligible institution/programme.

eligibility.question: Can you meet the programme's language requirement (e.g. IELTS/TOEFL for English-taught)?
eligibility.failMessage: You must meet the language requirement of the specific programme.

benefits: Tuition fee waived (small administrative fee may remain)
Available across Flemish universities and colleges

name: Aga Khan Foundation International Scholarship
summary: 50% grant / 50% loan awards for outstanding postgraduate students from selected developing countries who have no other means of financing their studies, usable at top universities worldwide.

eligibility.question: Are you from one of the eligible countries (mainly South/Central Asia, East Africa, Middle East)?
eligibility.failMessage: Eligibility is limited to nationals of the foundation's listed countries.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Can you document genuine financial need?
eligibility.failMessage: AKF funds students who cannot otherwise finance their study.

eligibility.question: Are you under 30?
eligibility.failMessage: Preference is given to applicants under 30.

benefits: Covers tuition and living costs (travel excluded)
Master's priority; PhD only in exceptional cases

deadlines.intake: 2027 cycle — country applications due around March 2027

source.name: Aga Khan Foundation — International Scholarship Programme

changeLog.note: Initial record seeded from the official source (machine-compiled; deadlines vary by year and country — confirm on the official page).

name: Joint Japan/World Bank Graduate Scholarship (JJ/WBGSP)
summary: Funds development-related Master's programmes at preferred universities worldwide for mid-career professionals from World Bank borrowing countries — tuition, stipend, travel and insurance.

eligibility.question: Are you a national of a World Bank member developing country?
eligibility.failMessage: Only nationals of eligible borrowing countries can apply.

eligibility.question: Do you hold (or will you soon complete) a Bachelor's degree?
eligibility.failMessage: A completed first degree (Bachelor's or equivalent) is required.

eligibility.question: Do you have 3+ years of paid development-related work experience?
eligibility.failMessage: At least three years of relevant professional experience is required.

eligibility.question: Do you have an unconditional admission to a participating Master's programme?
eligibility.failMessage: An admission letter from a preferred/participating programme is required before applying.

name: Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program
summary: Comprehensive scholarships — tuition, accommodation, travel, and leadership development — for academically talented young Africans facing financial barriers, at partner universities in Africa, North America and Europe.

eligibility.question: Are you a citizen of an African country?
eligibility.failMessage: The program primarily serves young people from Sub-Saharan Africa.

eligibility.question: Do you face significant financial barriers to education?
eligibility.failMessage: Demonstrated economic disadvantage is a core criterion.

eligibility.question: Are you a young person (most partners cap around 29 for Master's)?
eligibility.failMessage: Partner universities apply youth age limits.

eligibility.question: Have you applied for (or secured) admission to an eligible programme?
eligibility.failMessage: This award requires an application or offer at an eligible institution/programme.

benefits: Mentoring, leadership development and transition support
Apply via each partner university's own process

deadlines.intake: 2027 intakes — deadlines vary by partner university
